Mid to 4AM: 12:00, working engine full astern, attempting to back vessel off shoals. 1:22, stop. Awaiting favorable backing condition. 2:00, informed Headquarters by dispatch of grounding and requested assistance from Bering Sea Patrol Force.
3:45AM: Constant bearings made on Cape Prince of Wales, Light, school-house and right tangent of Point.

4AM to 8AM: Danger bearing of Wales Village 112 degrees true. Lead going constantly, and as rapidly as a competent leadsman could obtain soundings. Wales school house bore 100 degrees true, depth 13 fathoms. School house bore 98 degrees true, depth 9 fathoms. 4:11AM: vessel grounded on Prince of Wales Shoal. Backed full speed; then ahead full speed with left full rudder for a brief interval in order to separate ship and oomiak which had been coming together on account of screw currents. Again backed full speed. When it became apparent that vessel would not move, stopped engine. Soundings gave from 2.5 fathoms forward to 3 fathoms on starboard quarter.

8AM to Merid: Continued hauling on line to stream anchor, which failed to hold and was hoisted up on spanker boom. Made ready 2.25 ton port bower anchor, and at 9:55, slung between two whaleboats to carry out as a kedge made fast to 10 manila hawser.  Ten fifty five anchor let go about 200 yards. abreast stern quarter, slightly to starboard.
Noon: Cape Prince of Wales, Als.

Note: 4PM to 8PM: Kept heavy strain on hawser. 6:30, sounded alongside and found from 2.25 fathoms on port bow to 3.25 fathoms on starboard quarter. 7:00, again carried out 1700 lb. stream anchor with 250 fathoms of six inch line and planted it beyond the bower.

8PM to Mid.: Kept heavy strain on both kedges and backed full power whenever vessel showed signs of movement. Ship's head swung off to right slowly during watch until 11:30, when movement became erratic. Soundings at end of watch showed a half fathom less water than had previously been noted.

Mid to 4AM: 2:15, vessel buoyant. Made ready to heave astern and at 2:30 did so, heaving on kedges and occasional backing on engines. 2:45 hove short on both kedges. 2:30 taking kedges in and at 3:40 backing astern. Vessel fully afloat.
